subject: Human Resources
title: The Apprenticeship System
description: A 60-second lesson on The Apprenticeship System.

hook.text: A 12-year-old boy moves into a master carpenter's home. For seven years, he'll work for free—learning the trade.

buildup.text: Apprenticeship was the dominant model of workplace learning for centuries—live-in, hands-on, and years long.

discovery.text: Masters provided room, board, and instruction. Apprentices provided labor. It was training and employment combined.

twist.text: The system could be exploitative—apprentices had few rights and masters could be abusive without consequence.

climax.text: Modern internships and mentorship programs are direct descendants of the centuries-old apprenticeship model.

punchline.text: Learn by doing is the oldest job training method.

quiz.question: How did the apprenticeship system work?
options: Apprentices worked for masters in exchange for training and room
